title: circaea lutetiana
intro: Circaea lutetiana, also known as the wood sorrel or lesser wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species in the family Lythraceae. It is native to Europe and Western Asia, and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of ascorbic acid, also known as vitamin C. The medicinal properties of Circaea lutetiana include its ability to act as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which can help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. Herbal preparations of Circaea lutetiana, such as teas and infusions, can be used to treat a range of health issues, including colds and flu, digestive problems, and skin conditions, and are also believed to have anti-aging properties.
